Part of me feels obligated, but another part knows my career momentum right now is really important. I'm not sure how to navigate this without disappointing them.
scenario_brief: Aisha faces tension between supporting aging parents and maintaining her career trajectory.

trigger_text: U: I've been noticing that most of my friendships feel pretty surface-level. We grab drinks and talk about work, but I don't think any of my friends really know me. I wonder if I should invest more effort into building actual depth, or if that's just unrealistic for someone at my stage of life.
scenario_brief: Aisha questions whether to deepen her friendships or accept that work-focused relationships are sufficient.

trigger_text: U: I've been considering learning product management formally, but the time commitment feels risky when things are moving so fast at work right now.
scenario_brief: Aisha is contemplating investing time in formal product management training despite her demanding current role.

trigger_text: U: Everyone seems to be getting trained up on AI and automation in marketing. I'm starting to feel like I need to catch up before I fall too far behind.
scenario_brief: Aisha recognizes a trending skill gap in AI/automation and worries about losing her competitive edge.

trigger_text: U: I heard about this intensive workshop on behavioral psychology for marketers. It's outside my usual area, but I think it could give me an edge. I'm just not sure if it's the right moment to invest in it.
scenario_brief: Aisha discovers a specialized workshop that could differentiate her skills but questions its timing and value.

trigger_text: U: I've been invited to this exclusive wine-tasting event next weekend at a members-only club—it's supposed to be really selective—but the ticket price is honestly kind of steep.
scenario_brief: Considering attending exclusive wine-tasting event despite high ticket cost

trigger_text: U: I'm thinking about doing a complete apartment makeover with this minimalist aesthetic I keep seeing everywhere, but I'm worried I might lose interest in it after a few months.
scenario_brief: Weighing home renovation investment against concern about trend durability

trigger_text: U: I just heard about a pop-up art gallery featuring this emerging artist—it's only here for two weeks and it's been getting a ton of social media buzz. I'm interested, but timing feels off with work being crazy right now.
